sanskrit:
अथैतदप्यशक्तोऽसि कर्तुं मद्योगमाश्रितः।
सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं ततः कुरु यतात्मवान्॥
transliteration:
athaitad apy aśakto ’si kartuṃ mad-yogam āśritaḥ |
sarva-karma-phala-tyāgaṃ tataḥ kuru yatātmavān ||
literal_meaning: If even this you are unable to do, then, taking refuge in My yoga, perform the renunciation of all fruits of action, being self-controlled.
contextual_meaning: When full engagement in devotional practice feels impossible, shift from outcome to process: renounce the psychological payoff of success/failure and regulate the mind through detached action.
psychological_state: Performance-anxious but willing; needs graded exposure rather than all-or-nothing spirituality.
trigger_scenario: A burned-out caregiver who can’t join a meditation retreat but can donate the overtime pay to charity and mentally label it “not mine.”
anchor_text: Let go of the outcome and focus fully on the action itself.
